name: Biscotti Di Prato (Little Almond Cookies)
recipeInstructions:
Preheat the oven to 375°F. Toast both blanched and unblanched almonds on a cookie sheet for 15 minutes until golden brown.
Grind 4 ounces of mixed blanched and unblanched almonds very fine, then cut remaining toasted almonds into pieces.
Place flour in a mound on a pasta board; make a well in the center. Add sugar and eggs to the well and mix thoroughly.
Add salt, saffron, and baking soda; mix until all ingredients are well combined. Gradually incorporate flour until all but 2 tablespoons are mixed.
Knead dough for 10-15 minutes, then add finely ground almonds and almond pieces. Knead another 2-3 minutes.
Divide dough into 8 pieces. Shape each piece into a long, thin roll (about 3/4 inch diameter) and place on a buttered and floured cookie sheet.
Beat egg white slightly; brush tops of rolls with it using a pastry brush.
Bake at 375°F for 18-20 minutes. Remove from oven and cut rolls at 45-degree angle every 3/4 inch to form biscotti shape.
Place back in oven at 275°F for 35-45 minutes until very dry. Serve after 2-3 days; store in a paper bag or transfer to jar/can after a week.
